Tonifies the Spleen, augments qi, nourishes the Stomach, and generates fluids.

composition.explanation_zh: 益气和中，调和诸药；炙甘草
composition.explanation_en: Augments qi, harmonizes the middle, and harmonizes the other herbs (use honey-fried).

preparation_zh: 以水一斗二升，煮取六升，温服一升，日三夜一服。
preparation_en: Boil in twelve sheng of water down to six sheng; take one sheng warm, three times during the day and once at night.
description_zh: 张仲景《金匮要略》名方。治肺胃阴虚、气火上逆之肺痿与虚热咳嗽：咳唾涎沫、气短喘促、咽喉干燥、舌红少苔、脉虚数。全方以麦冬七升（今约60g）重用为君，配半夏一升辛润相成，再佐人参、甘草、大枣、粳米以培中生津。

rhyme.text_zh: 麦门冬汤用人参，枣草粳米半夏存。肺痿咳逆因虚火，清养肺胃此方珍。
rhyme.attribution: 方剂学方歌（传统汤头歌诀）

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Zhang Zhongjing's formula from the Jin Gui Yao Lue for Lung-Stomach yin vacuity with qi-fire counterflow causing Lung atrophy and vacuity-heat cough. Mai dong is used in a very large dose as chief, combined with a small amount of ban xia for acrid-moist complementarity, plus ren shen, gan cao, and da zao to cultivate the middle and generate fluids.

treatment_principle.en: Reduce food accumulation and harmonize the Stomach

composition.explanation_zh: 善消肉食油腻之积，为君药
composition.explanation_en: Excels at dispersing meat and greasy food stagnation — sovereign herb.

composition.explanation_zh: 下气消食，长于消谷面之积
composition.explanation_en: Descends qi and disperses food stagnation, particularly grains and flour products.
Dries dampness, transforms phlegm, harmonizes the Stomach, and descends counterflow.
Regulates qi, harmonizes the Stomach, transforms dampness, and arouses the Spleen.
Strengthens the Spleen, percolates dampness, and harmonizes the middle to stop diarrhea.

composition.explanation_zh: 食积易郁而化热，连翘清其郁热
composition.explanation_en: Food stagnation readily generates heat; lian qiao clears this constraint-heat.

preparation_zh: 上为末，炊饼为丸，如梧桐子大，每服七八十丸，食远白汤下。
preparation_en: Grind into powder, form into pills using steamed bread as binder, the size of a wutong seed; take seventy to eighty pills per dose, on an empty stomach with plain hot water.
description_zh: 朱丹溪《丹溪心法》名方。治一切食积证：脘腹痞满胀痛、嗳腐吞酸、恶食呕逆、大便泄泻、舌苔厚腻、脉滑。全方以山楂为君消肉积，神曲消酒食陈腐之积，莱菔子消谷面之积——三味分消不同食积。临床仍当按原方七味投药。

rhyme.text_zh: 保和神曲与山楂，苓夏陈翘菔子加。炊饼为丸淡水下，食积成滞用之佳。
rhyme.attribution: 方剂学方歌（传统汤头歌诀）

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Zhu Danxi's formula from Dan Xi Xin Fa for comprehensive food stagnation. Shan zha digests meat, shen qu digests alcohol and stale foods, lai fu zi digests grains and starches—the three together address all types of dietary accumulation. Combined with ban xia, chen pi, fu ling, and lian qiao for qi-moving, phlegm-transforming, heat-clearing support.

treatment_principle.en: Warm the viscera and calm roundworms

composition.explanation_zh: 味极酸，蛔得酸则静，为君药
composition.explanation_en: Extremely sour — roundworms become still when they taste sourness; sovereign herb.
Pungent and warm to disperse cold; roundworms become suppressed when they taste pungency.
Warms the middle and disperses cold, warming the viscera to calm roundworms.
Warms the Kidney and Spleen, assisting gan jiang in warming the viscera.
Unblocks yang, disperses cold, and warms channels and vessels.
Bitter and cold to clear heat; roundworms descend when they taste bitterness.

composition.explanation_zh: 苦寒清热燥湿，与黄连同泄上热
composition.explanation_en: Bitter and cold; clears heat and dries dampness, with huang lian draining upper heat.
Greatly tonifies original qi to restore lower vacuity.

composition.explanation_zh: 养血和血，合人参补气养血
composition.explanation_en: Nourishes and harmonizes blood; with ren shen augments qi and nourishes blood.

preparation_zh: 异捣筛，合治之。以苦酒渍乌梅一宿，去核，蒸之五斗米下，饭熟捣成泥，和药令相得，内臼中，与蜜杵二千下，丸如梧桐子大。先食饮服十丸，日三服，稍加至二十丸。禁生冷、滑物、臭食等。
preparation_en: Pound and sieve each ingredient separately, then combine. Soak wu mei in vinegar overnight, remove the pits, steam below five dou of rice; when the rice is cooked, pound into a paste, blend with the other ingredients until well mixed, place in a mortar, and pound with honey two thousand times to form pills the size of a wutong seed. Take ten pills before meals with a drink, three times daily, gradually increasing to twenty pills. Avoid raw, cold, slippery, and foul-smelling foods.
description_zh: 张仲景《伤寒论·厥阴病篇》第338条方，为驱虫剂与厥阴病寒热错杂证之代表方。治蛔厥：脘腹阵痛、烦闷呕吐、时发时止、得食则吐、甚则吐蛔、手足厥冷；亦治久泻久痢属寒热错杂者。方中酸（乌梅）、辛（细辛、桂枝、附子、干姜、川椒）、苦（黄连、黄柏）三味并用，寒热并调；人参、当归补气养血以固本。

rhyme.text_zh: 乌梅丸用细辛桂，黄连黄柏及当归。人参椒姜加附子，清上温下又安蛔。
rhyme.attribution: 方剂学方歌（传统汤头歌诀）

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Zhang Zhongjing's formula from the Shang Han Lun jue yin chapter (line 338), serving as both an antiparasitic and a representative formula for jue yin cold-heat complex patterns. It treats roundworm reversal (hui jue) with paroxysmal epigastric pain, vomiting after eating, and vomiting of worms, as well as chronic diarrhea and dysentery of the cold-heat complex type. The formula combines sour (wu mei), pungent (xi xin, gui zhi, fu zi, gan jiang), and bitter (huang lian, huang bai) with qi and blood supplementation.

treatment_principle.en: Augment qi and secure the exterior; astringe yin and stop sweating

composition.explanation_zh: 甘涩平，专入肺经，固表止汗，为君；与麻黄茎发汗作用相反
composition.explanation_en: Sweet, astringent, and neutral — enters only the Lung channel; secures the exterior and stops sweating as sovereign. Pharmacologically opposite to the stem of Ephedra, which promotes sweating.

composition.explanation_zh: 咸涩微寒，敛阴潜阳，固涩止汗，与麻黄根相须为用
composition.explanation_en: Salty, astringent, and slightly cool; astringes yin, subdues yang, and secures against sweating — paired with Ma Huang Gen as mutual reinforcement.
Augments qi and secures the exterior, tonifying defensive qi vacuity.

preparation_zh: 上三味为粗散，每服三钱，水一盏半，小麦百余粒，同煎至八分，去滓热服，日二服，不以时候。
preparation_en: Grind the three ingredients into a coarse powder; take three qian per dose with one and a half cups of water and over one hundred grains of wheat, decoct down to eight-tenths, strain the dregs and take hot, twice daily at no fixed time.
description_zh: 宋《太平惠民和剂局方》名方。治诸虚不足、体常自汗、夜卧尤甚、久而不止、心悸惊惕、短气烦倦之卫外不固、阴液外泄证。原方四味：牡蛎、黄芪、麻黄根、浮小麦。临床应用当按原方全方投药。

rhyme.text_zh: 牡蛎散内用黄芪，小麦麻黄根合宜。卫虚自汗或盗汗，固表收涩效可期。
rhyme.attribution: 方剂学方歌（传统汤头歌诀）

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: From Tai Ping Hui Min He Ji Ju Fang, this formula treats generalized vacuity with spontaneous sweating that worsens at night, palpitations, shortness of breath, and lassitude—from exterior insecurity with yin fluid leaking outward. The original formula contains mu li, huang qi, ma huang gen, and fu xiao mai. This data entry is structurally simplified; clinical use requires the complete original formula.

treatment_principle.en: Clear Stomach heat and enrich Kidney yin

composition.explanation_zh: 辛甘大寒，清阳明有余之火，为君药
composition.explanation_en: Pungent, sweet, and very cold; clears exuberant fire of the Yang Ming — sovereign herb.

composition.explanation_zh: 滋肾水之不足，壮水以制火，为臣药
composition.explanation_en: Enriches insufficient Kidney water; strengthens water to control fire — minister herb.

composition.explanation_zh: 苦寒质润，助石膏清胃火，又助熟地滋肾阴
composition.explanation_en: Bitter, cold, and moistening; assists shi gao in clearing Stomach fire and helps shu di nourish Kidney yin.
Nourishes yin, clears heat, moistens the Lung, and generates fluids.

composition.explanation_zh: 引热下行，兼补肝肾，使上炎之火循经下降
composition.explanation_en: Directs heat downward and also tonifies Liver and Kidney, leading blazing fire to descend along the channels.

preparation_zh: 水一钟半，煎七分，温服或冷服。
preparation_en: Decoct one and a half cups of water down to seven-tenths; take warm or cold.
description_zh: 明·张景岳《景岳全书·新方八阵》名方。治胃热阴虚证：牙痛齿衄、烦热干渴、舌红苔黄而干；亦治消渴、口舌生疮属胃热阴虚者。全方清胃热与滋肾阴并行，上病下取，水火并治。

rhyme.text_zh: 玉女煎用熟地黄，膏知牛膝麦冬襄。阴亏胃热相为病，牙痛齿衄宜煎尝。
rhyme.attribution: 方剂学方歌（传统汤头歌诀）

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Zhang Jingyue's formula from Jing Yue Quan Shu Xin Fang Ba Zhen for Stomach-heat yin-vacuity syndrome with toothache, gingival bleeding, vexing heat, dry mouth, and a red tongue with dry yellow coating. It treats above by draining below, simultaneously clearing Stomach heat (shi gao, zhi mu) and enriching Kidney yin (shu di, mai dong), with niu xi directing the action downward.

treatment_principle.en: Promote urination and leach out dampness; warm yang and transform qi

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡渗利膀胱水湿，与泽泻相须为用共为君药，专司利水消肿
composition.explanation_en: Sweet and bland, percolates water and dampness directly through the Bladder — paired with Ze Xie as co-sovereign, solely responsible for draining water and reducing edema.

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡渗湿，直达膀胱，利水作用最强，为君药
composition.explanation_en: Sweet and bland, leaches dampness directly through the Bladder — the strongest diuretic in the formula, serving as sovereign.

composition.explanation_zh: 健脾渗湿，助泽泻利水消肿
composition.explanation_en: Fortifies the Spleen and percolates dampness, assisting Ze Xie in promoting urination.

composition.explanation_zh: 健脾燥湿，助脾运化水湿
composition.explanation_en: Strengthens the Spleen and dries dampness, restoring the Spleen's transporting function.

composition.explanation_zh: 温阳化气，解太阳之表，使膀胱气化复常而水湿得行
composition.explanation_en: Warms yang and transforms qi, releasing the Taiyang exterior and restoring the Bladder's qi-transformation so fluids can move.

preparation_zh: 上五味，捣为散，以白饮和服方寸匕，日三服，多饮暖水，汗出愈。
preparation_en: Pound all five ingredients into a powder; take one square-inch spoonful (fang cun bi) mixed with plain hot water, three times daily; drink warm water copiously — resolution comes with sweating.
description_zh: 《伤寒论》五苓散为太阳蓄水证而设，以表邪未解、内传太阳之腑、膀胱气化失司，见小便不利、头痛微热、烦渴欲饮、水入即吐（水逆证）为主。为利水渗湿、温阳化气之祖方。原方由泽泻、猪苓、茯苓、白术、桂枝五味组成。

rhyme.text_zh: 五苓散治太阳腑，泽泻白术与猪苓。茯苓桂枝温化饮，利水渗湿效堪珍。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: The Shang Han Lun formula for greater yang water-amassing (xu shui) syndrome, with impaired Bladder qi transformation causing inhibited urination, headache, mild fever, vexing thirst with immediate vomiting after drinking (water counterflow). The ancestral formula for promoting urination, percolating dampness, and warming yang to transform qi, composed of ze xie, zhu ling, fu ling, bai zhu, and gui zhi.

treatment_principle.en: Promote urination, nourish yin, and clear heat

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡渗利膀胱水湿，为利水专品而不伤正，是本方同名君药
composition.explanation_en: Sweet and bland, percolates water through the Bladder without damaging upright qi — the eponymous sovereign of this formula.

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡利水渗湿，助猪苓利小便、导水热下行
composition.explanation_en: Sweet and bland, assists Zhu Ling in promoting urination and directing damp-heat downward.
Strengthens the Spleen and percolates dampness, reinforcing the diuretic action.
Clears heat and promotes urination, unblocks painful dribbling and alleviates thirst.

composition.explanation_zh: 滋阴润燥止血，使利水而不伤阴，为本方关键之佐药
composition.explanation_en: Enriches yin and moistens dryness, ensuring that the diuretics do not further deplete yin — the pivotal counterbalancing assistant.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。原方五味（猪苓、茯苓、泽泻、滑石、阿胶），其中阿胶另烊化兑服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally. The original formula has five ingredients (zhu ling, fu ling, ze xie, hua shi, e jiao); e jiao is melted separately and blended into the decoction.
description_zh: 《伤寒论》猪苓汤主治阳明病、少阴病水热互结伤阴证，证见小便不利、发热口渴、心烦不寐，或咳或呕，或下利。与五苓散同属利水之剂，但本方重在利水清热兼养阴，适于阴虚有热之水停；原方以猪苓、茯苓、泽泻、滑石、阿胶五味配伍。

rhyme.text_zh: 猪苓汤用猪苓先，泽泻阿胶滑石研。茯苓四味成方法，热病伤阴此方痊。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: The Shang Han Lun formula for water-heat mutual binding with yin damage in yang-brightness and lesser yin disease, presenting as inhibited urination, fever, thirst, and vexing insomnia. Unlike Wu Ling San, this formula emphasizes promoting urination and clearing heat while concurrently nourishing yin, making it suitable for yin-vacuity heat patterns with water stagnation. Composed of zhu ling, fu ling, ze xie, hua shi, and e jiao.

treatment_principle.en: Clear heat, promote urination, resolve dampness, and reduce jaundice

composition.explanation_zh: 苦微寒，清利湿热，为治黄疸之要药，为君
composition.explanation_en: Bitter and slightly cold, the principal herb for clearing damp-heat jaundice — the sovereign.

composition.explanation_zh: 清利三焦湿热，导湿热从小便而去
composition.explanation_en: Clears damp-heat from all three burners, directing it out through urination.

composition.explanation_zh: 泻热通便，使瘀热从大便而下
composition.explanation_en: Drains heat and unblocks the bowels, moving stagnant heat out through the stool.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。以水一斗二升，先煮茵陈减六升，内二味，煮取三升，去滓，分三服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally. Boil yin chen hao in twelve sheng of water down to six sheng, add the other two ingredients, decoct down to three sheng, strain the dregs, and divide into three doses.
description_zh: 《伤寒论》茵陈蒿汤为治阳黄之祖方。主治湿热黄疸阳黄证，一身面目俱黄、黄色鲜明如橘子色，腹微满、口渴、小便短赤、大便不爽或秘结，舌苔黄腻，脉沉实或滑数。三味合用，二便分消，湿热并治，使瘀热湿邪从前后二阴分消而黄疸得退。

rhyme.text_zh: 茵陈蒿汤治阳黄，栀子大黄组成方。湿热相伴发黄疸，清热利湿退黄良。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: The Shang Han Lun ancestral formula for yang-type jaundice (yang huang). It treats damp-heat jaundice with bright yellow discoloration of the whole body and face resembling tangerine color, epigastric fullness, thirst, short dark urine, and a yellow greasy tongue coating. Its three herbs work together to eliminate stasis-heat and dampness through both stool and urine, resolving jaundice by draining damp-heat via the anterior and posterior pathways.

treatment_principle.en: Diffuse and regulate qi dynamics; clear heat and resolve dampness

composition.explanation_zh: 苦辛宣降上焦肺气，气化则湿亦化，为上仁
composition.explanation_en: Bitter and acrid, diffuses and descends Lung qi in the upper burner — when qi moves, dampness transforms.

composition.explanation_zh: 辛温芳香，化湿行气醒脾，畅中焦气机，为中仁，与杏仁、薏苡仁共成三仁之制
composition.explanation_en: Acrid, warming, and aromatic — transforms dampness, moves qi, awakens the Spleen, and unblocks the middle burner; the middle 'kernel' of the classical trio with Xing Ren and Yi Yi Ren.

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡渗利下焦湿热，为下仁
composition.explanation_en: Sweet and bland, leaches damp-heat through the lower burner.

composition.explanation_zh: 清热利湿，使湿热从小便而去
composition.explanation_en: Clears heat and promotes urination, directing damp-heat out through the urine.

composition.explanation_zh: 燥湿化痰降逆，助畅中焦
composition.explanation_en: Dries dampness and transforms phlegm, descending counterflow to unblock the middle burner.
Moves qi, transforms dampness, and eliminates fullness.

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡渗湿，通利小便，导湿热从小便而出
composition.explanation_en: Sweet and bland; leaches dampness and promotes urination, guiding damp-heat out through the urine.

composition.explanation_zh: 甘淡微寒，清心除烦，利尿通淋，助滑石、通草清利湿热
composition.explanation_en: Sweet, bland, and slightly cool; clears Heart heat, eliminates vexation, and promotes urination, assisting Hua Shi and Tong Cao to clear damp-heat.

preparation_zh: 水八碗，煮取三碗，每服一碗，日三服。
preparation_en: Boil eight bowls of water down to three bowls; take one bowl per dose, three times daily.
description_zh: 《温病条辨》三仁汤为湿温初起及暑温挟湿之主方。证见头痛恶寒、身重疼痛、午后身热、面色淡黄、胸闷不饥、舌白不渴、脉弦细而濡。以杏、蔻、薏三仁宣上、畅中、渗下，配半夏、厚朴、滑石等分消湿热于三焦，使气机畅通、湿去热清。方义体现吴鞠通「治上焦如羽，治中焦如衡，治下焦如权」之旨。

rhyme.text_zh: 三仁杏蔻薏苡仁，朴夏白通滑竹伦。水用甘澜扬百遍，湿温初起法堪遵。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Wu Jutong's formula from Wen Bing Tiao Bian for early-stage damp-warmth and summerheat-dampness. The 'three kernels'—xing ren, bai dou kou, and yi yi ren—diffuse the upper burner, open the middle burner, and percolate the lower burner respectively, combined with ban xia, hou po, hua shi, and others to separate and discharge damp-heat across all three burners. This embodies Wu Jutong's principle: 'Treat the upper like a feather, the middle like a balance, the lower like a weight.'

name_en: Agastache Powder to Rectify the Qi

treatment_principle.en: Release the exterior and transform dampness; regulate qi and harmonize the middle

composition.explanation_zh: 芳香化湿，解表和中，辟秽止呕，为君
composition.explanation_en: Aromatically transforms dampness, releases the exterior, harmonizes the middle and stops vomiting — the sovereign herb.

composition.explanation_zh: 发散风寒，助藿香解表散寒
composition.explanation_en: Disperses wind-cold, assisting Huo Xiang in releasing the exterior.
Disperses wind-cold and dries dampness.

composition.explanation_zh: 燥湿化痰，降逆止呕（代半夏曲）
composition.explanation_en: Dries dampness, transforms phlegm, and directs rebellious qi downward (substituting for Ban Xia Qu).
Regulates qi and dries dampness, harmonizing the Stomach.
Moves qi, dries dampness, and eliminates fullness.
Strengthens the Spleen and percolates dampness.
Fortifies the Spleen, dries dampness, and harmonizes the middle burner.

composition.explanation_zh: 宣肺利膈，助藿香苏叶宣散表邪
composition.explanation_en: Diffuses Lung qi and opens the chest, assisting the exterior-releasing herbs.
Harmonizes the actions of the other herbs and augments middle qi.
Harmonizes the Stomach, stops vomiting, and assists the exterior-releasing action.

composition.explanation_zh: 和中益气，与生姜相配调和营卫
composition.explanation_en: Harmonizes the middle and augments qi; paired with Sheng Jiang to harmonize ying and wei.

preparation_zh: 上为细末，每服二钱，水一盏，姜钱三片，枣一枚，同煎至七分，热服。
preparation_en: Grind into fine powder; take two qian per dose with one cup of water, three slices of ginger coins, and one jujube, decocted down to seven-tenths; take hot.
description_zh: 《太平惠民和剂局方》藿香正气散主治外感风寒、内伤湿滞之证，证见恶寒发热、头痛、胸膈满闷、脘腹疼痛、恶心呕吐、肠鸣泄泻、舌苔白腻。表里同治、升降并调，为解表化湿、理气和中之代表方，尤宜于夏月外感兼内伤生冷之「阴暑」及暑湿吐泻。

rhyme.text_zh: 藿香正气腹皮苏，甘桔陈苓术朴俱。夏曲白芷加姜枣，感伤岚瘴并能驱。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: From Tai Ping Hui Min He Ji Ju Fang, this formula treats exterior wind-cold with interior dampness stagnation causing chills, fever, headache, chest oppression, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea with a white greasy tongue coating. It simultaneously releases the exterior and transforms dampness, regulates qi and harmonizes the middle, and is particularly suited for summer 'yin-summerheat' with concurrent external contraction and internal cold-dampness dietary injury.

source_chapter: 《备急千金要方》卷八 诸风

treatment_principle.zh: 祛风湿，止痹痛，补肝肾，益气血
treatment_principle.en: Dispel wind-dampness and relieve painful obstruction; tonify Liver and Kidney; augment qi and blood

composition.explanation_zh: 辛温通达，善祛下焦及筋骨间风寒湿邪，为君
composition.explanation_en: Acrid and warming, penetrates to the lower burner and sinews to expel wind-cold-damp — the sovereign.
Dispels wind-damp, tonifies Liver and Kidney, and strengthens sinews and bones.
Tonifies Liver and Kidney and strengthens sinews and bones.

composition.explanation_zh: 补肝肾，强筋骨，引药下行
composition.explanation_en: Tonifies Liver and Kidney, strengthens sinews, and directs the other herbs downward.

composition.explanation_zh: 搜剔筋骨之风寒湿邪，通络止痛
composition.explanation_en: Penetrates deeply to expel wind-cold-damp from sinews and bones, unblocks the collaterals and stops pain.
Dispels wind-damp, relaxes sinews and collaterals, clears vacuity heat.

composition.explanation_zh: 祛风胜湿，为风药之润剂
composition.explanation_en: Dispels wind and overcomes dampness — a moistening wind-dispelling herb.

composition.explanation_zh: 温经散寒止痛，助祛寒湿
composition.explanation_en: Warms the channels, disperses cold and stops pain, assisting the expulsion of cold-damp.
Nourishes and invigorates blood.
Invigorates blood, moves qi, and stops pain.
Enriches Liver and Kidney, nourishes blood and replenishes essence.
Nourishes blood and harmonizes the nutritive level, softens the Liver and stops pain.
Greatly tonifies original qi and fortifies the Spleen.
Strengthens the Spleen and percolates dampness.
Harmonizes the actions of the other herbs and augments qi.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally.
description_zh: 孙思邈《备急千金要方》独活寄生汤为痹证日久、肝肾两亏、气血不足之代表方。主治腰膝疼痛、痿软不能屈伸，或麻木不仁、畏寒喜温、心悸气短、舌淡苔白、脉细弱。本方扶正祛邪并用，祛风湿、止痹痛与补肝肾、益气血并行，为后世治慢性风湿痹证之祖方。

rhyme.text_zh: 独活寄生艽防辛，芎归地芍桂苓均。杜仲牛膝人参草，冷风顽痹屈能伸。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Sun Simiao's formula from Bei Ji Qian Jin Yao Fang for chronic impediment (bi) with Liver-Kidney vacuity and qi-blood insufficiency. It treats lumbar-knee pain, weakness, inability to flex or extend, numbness, aversion to cold, palpitations, shortness of breath, a pale tongue with white coating, and a fine weak pulse. It uses du huo, sang ji sheng, du zhong, and fourteen other herbs to simultaneously dispel wind-dampness, stop impediment pain, supplement the Liver-Kidney, and augment qi-blood.

treatment_principle.en: Regulate qi and transform phlegm; harmonize the Stomach and benefit the Gallbladder

composition.explanation_zh: 燥湿化痰，降逆和胃，为君
composition.explanation_en: Dries dampness, transforms phlegm, descends counterflow and harmonizes the Stomach — the sovereign.
Clears heat, transforms phlegm, relieves irritability and stops vomiting.
Moves qi and disperses phlegm so that phlegm descends with qi.
Regulates qi and dries dampness, assisting in phlegm transformation and Stomach harmonization.
Strengthens the Spleen and percolates dampness, addressing the source of phlegm.
Harmonizes the actions of the other herbs and augments middle qi.
Harmonizes the Stomach and descends counterflow, assisting phlegm transformation and anti-emetic action.
Augments qi, harmonizes the middle and the other herbs.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally.
description_zh: 陈言《三因极一病证方论》温胆汤实为治胆郁痰扰之良方，虽名「温胆」而药性偏凉平，主治胆胃不和、痰热内扰，证见虚烦不眠、触事易惊、或呕吐呃逆、癫痫惊悸、舌苔白腻或黄腻、脉弦滑。本方由二陈汤加枳实、竹茹变化而来，为后世治痰热扰心诸证之基础方。

rhyme.text_zh: 温胆汤中苓半草，枳竹陈皮加姜枣。虚烦不眠证多端，此系胆虚痰上扰。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: From Chen Yan's San Yin Ji Yi Bing Zheng Fang Lun, despite the name 'Warm the Gallbladder,' the formula's nature is actually cool and balanced. It treats Gallbladder-Stomach disharmony with phlegm-heat internal disturbance causing vacuity vexation and insomnia, susceptibility to fright, nausea, hiccup, epilepsy, palpitations, and a slippery wiry pulse with a white or yellow greasy tongue coating. Built from Er Chen Tang with the addition of zhi shi and zhu ru.

treatment_principle.en: Moisten the Lung and clear heat; regulate qi and transform phlegm

composition.explanation_zh: 润肺清热化痰，善治燥痰，为君
composition.explanation_en: Moistens the Lung, clears heat, and transforms dry phlegm — the sovereign herb.
Clears heat and moistens dryness, sweeps out phlegm and dissipates nodules.

composition.explanation_zh: 健脾渗湿以杜生痰之源
composition.explanation_en: Strengthens the Spleen and percolates dampness, addressing the source of phlegm generation.
Regulates qi and transforms phlegm (substituting for Ju Hong, which is not in the database).
Diffuses Lung qi, expels phlegm, and carries the other herbs upward.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。原方尚含天花粉，临床须按原方补入天花粉约2.4g。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally. The original formula also contains tian hua fen; in clinical practice, approximately 2.4g of tian hua fen should be added per the original formula.
description_zh: 程钟龄《医学心悟》贝母瓜蒌散为燥痰咳嗽之代表方。主治燥痰咳嗽、咽喉干燥哽痛、咳痰不爽、涩而难出、苔白而干。以润肺化痰之贝母、瓜蒌为主，佐以理气化痰、健脾渗湿之品，润燥相兼，标本并治。

rhyme.text_zh: 贝母瓜蒌散茯苓，橘红桔梗天花生。燥痰咳嗽咽喉痛，润肺清热化痰行。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Cheng Zhongling's formula from Yi Xue Xin Wu for dryness-phlegm cough. It treats dry cough with difficult expectoration, sticky phlegm that is hard to expel, a dry scratchy throat, and a white dry tongue coating. Moistening and phlegm-transforming bei mu and gua lou serve as the principal herbs, supported by qi-moving, phlegm-transforming, and Spleen-strengthening agents to address both root and branch.

name_pinyin: Bàn Xià Bái Zhú Tiān Má Tāng

treatment_principle.en: Transform phlegm and extinguish wind; fortify the Spleen and dispel dampness

composition.explanation_en: Dries dampness, transforms phlegm, and descends counterflow — co-sovereign with Tian Ma.

composition.explanation_zh: 平肝熄风，为治风痰眩晕头痛之要药
composition.explanation_en: Calms the Liver and extinguishes wind — the principal herb for wind-phlegm dizziness and headache.
Strengthens the Spleen and dries dampness, addressing the source of phlegm.
Strengthens the Spleen and percolates dampness.
Regulates qi and transforms phlegm, ensuring that when qi flows, phlegm resolves.
Harmonizes the actions of the other herbs and augments middle qi.
Harmonizes the Stomach and descends counterflow, assisting phlegm transformation.
Augments qi and harmonizes the middle, regulating ying and wei.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally.
description_zh: 程钟龄《医学心悟》半夏白术天麻汤为风痰上扰之眩晕头痛代表方。主治风痰上扰证：眩晕头痛、胸闷呕恶、舌苔白腻、脉弦滑。以二陈汤（半夏、陈皮、茯苓、甘草）健脾化痰为底，加白术以强健脾燥湿、天麻以平肝熄风，标本兼顾。《脾胃论》李东垣亦有同名方而组成略异，本条据程钟龄方。

rhyme.text_zh: 半夏白术天麻汤，苓草橘红大枣姜。眩晕头痛风痰证，痰化风熄复康强。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

description_en: Cheng Zhongling's formula from Yi Xue Xin Wu for wind-phlegm ascending disturbance causing dizziness and headache. Built on Er Chen Tang (ban xia, chen pi, fu ling, gan cao) as a Spleen-strengthening phlegm-transforming base, it adds bai zhu to further dry dampness and strengthen the Spleen, and tian ma to pacify the Liver and extinguish wind—treating both root and branch. Li Dongyuan also has a formula of the same name with slightly different composition.

treatment_principle.en: Induce sweating, dispel dampness, and simultaneously clear interior heat

composition.explanation_zh: 发散风寒，祛湿止痛，为君药
composition.explanation_en: Disperses wind-cold and eliminates dampness to relieve pain — the sovereign herb.
Dispels wind, overcomes dampness, and disperses cold to relieve pain.

composition.explanation_zh: 发汗祛湿，助羌活散寒除湿
composition.explanation_en: Induces sweating and eliminates dampness, assisting Qiang Huo.
Dispels wind-cold and opens the nasal passages.
Dispels wind-cold, dries dampness, and relieves pain.
Invigorates blood, dispels wind, moves qi, and stops pain.

composition.explanation_zh: 清热凉血，防诸辛温药过于温燥
composition.explanation_en: Clears heat and cools the blood, preventing excessive warmth and dryness from the other herbs.
Clears and drains interior heat.
Harmonizes the actions of all the herbs.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally.
description_zh: 九味羌活汤为外感风寒湿邪、内有蕴热之证的常用方。方以分经论治思想组方，以羌活为君，配伍防风、苍术等辛温解表药，佐以生地、黄芩清里热，体现了表里同治的特点。
description_en: A widely used formula for exterior wind-cold-dampness with latent interior heat. Built on the concept of treating by channel, it pairs Qiang Huo with wind-dispersing, dampness-resolving herbs while adding Sheng Di Huang and Huang Qin to clear interior heat — an exemplar of simultaneous exterior-interior treatment.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

treatment_principle.en: Dissipate cold and eliminate dampness while augmenting qi and releasing the exterior

composition.explanation_en: Dispels wind-cold, eliminates dampness, and relieves pain.
Dispels wind-dampness and stops impediment pain.
Releases the muscle layer, vents the exterior, and disperses heat.
Disperses wind-heat and clears the head and eyes.
Invigorates blood, moves qi, dispels wind, and stops pain.
Opens the Lung, benefits the throat, and guides the formula upward.
Regulates qi and expands the chest.
Fortifies the Spleen and percolates dampness.

composition.explanation_zh: 益气扶正，鼓邪外出，为"逆流挽舟"之用
composition.explanation_en: Augments qi and supports the upright, driving out the pathogen — the 'upstream rescue' strategy.
Harmonizes the actions of all the herbs.

preparation_zh: 水煎服，加生姜、薄荷少许同煎。
preparation_en: Decoct in water with a small amount of fresh ginger and mint; take orally.
description_zh: 人参败毒散是治疗气虚外感风寒湿证的代表方。方中以人参扶助正气，寓补于散，配伍羌活、独活等祛风散寒除湿药，体现了"逆流挽舟"法，使正气充则邪自去。
description_en: The representative formula for wind-cold-dampness exterior patterns complicated by qi vacuity. Ren Shen supports the upright qi while Qiang Huo and Du Huo dispel wind-cold-dampness, exemplifying the 'upstream rescue' method — once upright qi is sufficient, pathogens are naturally expelled.

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

name_en: Bupleurum and Kudzu Decoction to Release the Muscle Layer

treatment_principle.en: Release the muscle layer with cool-acrid herbs while clearing interior heat

composition.explanation_zh: 解肌透邪，疏泄少阳之热
composition.explanation_en: Releases the muscle layer and vents pathogenic heat from the Shaoyang.
Releases the muscle layer, abates fever, generates fluids, and relaxes sinews.
Clears and drains interior heat.
Nourishes blood and constrains yin.
Disperses wind-cold in the Taiyang channel.
Disperses wind-cold in the Yangming channel.
Clears and drains Yangming heat.
Opens the Lung and benefits the throat.
Harmonizes the actions of all the herbs.
Harmonizes the nutritive and defensive qi.
Releases the exterior and disperses cold.

preparation_zh: 水煎服。
preparation_en: Decoct in water and take orally.
description_zh: 柴葛解肌汤治外感风寒、邪郁化热之证，为三阳合病的常用方。以柴胡、葛根解肌透邪为君，配伍黄芩、石膏清内热，羌活、白芷散外寒，兼顾表里三阳。
description_en: Treats exterior wind-cold that has depressed and transformed into heat, a classic formula for combined disease of the three yang. Chai Hu and Ge Gen release the muscle layer as sovereigns, supported by Huang Qin and Shi Gao to clear interior heat while Qiang Huo and Bai Zhi scatter exterior cold — addressing all three yang simultaneously.

description_en: The representative formula for clearing heat and cooling the blood. Originally using rhinoceros horn as sovereign (now substituted by water buffalo horn), its four herbs combine heat-clearing blood-cooling with blood-invigorating stasis-dispersing actions, ensuring that heat is cleared and blood is calmed without the risk of congealing stasis. Indicated for various bleeding patterns caused by heat entering the blood level.

description_en: From Qian Yi's Craft of Medicinal Treatment for Childhood Disease Patterns, this formula treats Heart channel heat or Heart heat that has shifted downward to the Small Intestine. Sheng Di Huang clears heat and nourishes yin while Mu Tong and Zhu Ye clear the Heart and promote urination, guiding Heart fire out through the urine — hence the name 'Guide Out the Red.'

Huang Lian's bitter cold directly subdues Stomach fire as sovereign, while Sheng Ma raises and disperses depressed fire. Sheng Di Huang and Mu Dan Pi cool the blood, and Dang Gui invigorates blood to relieve pain.

A large dose of Bai Shao harmonizes the nutritive and stops pain as sovereign, combined with Huang Lian and Huang Qin to clear heat and dry dampness. Da Huang purges to guide out stagnation, Mu Xiang and Bing Lang move qi, and a trace of Rou Gui warms the vessels to prevent cold congelation — embodying the principle 'regulate qi and tenesmus resolves; move blood and purulent stool heals.'

Bai Tou Weng clears heat-toxins and cools the blood as sovereign, joined by three bitter-cold herbs — Huang Lian, Huang Bai, and Qin Pi — that clear heat, dry dampness, and resolve toxins together.

It integrates heat-clearing toxin-resolving, blood-invigorating stasis-transforming, and swelling-reducing nodule-dispersing actions so that heat-toxins clear, qi and blood flow, and swelling and pain resolve.

It omits Mang Xiao's softening and moistening action and reduces Da Huang's dose while decocting it together (not adding it later), yielding a gentler purgative. Suitable for mild Yangming bowel repletion with predominant focal distension and fullness rather than severe dryness and hardness.

Indicated for Yangming dryness-heat binding internally with predominant dryness and hardness but without focal distension.

Three harsh water-driving herbs — Gan Sui, Da Ji, and Yuan Hua — form the core, tempered by ten jujubes that augment qi and protect the Stomach. Indicated for repletion patterns of copious water-rheum such as suspended rheum and edema.

description_en: From the Shang Han Lun, this treats counterflow cold of the extremities due to yang constraint (not yang vacuity). Chai Hu raises and disperses to vent pathogens, Bai Shao constrains yin and harmonizes the nutritive, Zhi Shi moves qi and breaks binds, and Gan Cao harmonizes — together they course the Liver, regulate the Spleen, and free constrained yang. The foundational formula for Liver-Spleen harmonization.

Qing Hao and Huang Qin clear and vent Shaoyang damp-heat as sovereigns, supported by Ban Xia and Chen Pi to harmonize the Stomach and transform phlegm, Zhu Ru to clear heat and stop vomiting, Zhi Shi to move qi, and Fu Ling with Hua Shi to percolate dampness — the standard formula for Shaoyang patterns in warm disease.
